acceptable_use_policy: Data is used for law enforcement purposes only. Data is owned by the Kensington Police Department and is never sold to 3rd parties.
prohibited_uses: Immigration enforcement, harassment or intimidation, usage based solely on a protected class (i.e. race, sex, religion), Personal use, First Amendment Rights, Reproductive Health.
access_policy: Flock data is only retained for 30 days. However, an audit trail of the users who accessed the system is stored indefinitely.
hotlist_policy: Hotlist hits (i.e. stolen vehicles, wanted vehicles, missing persons, etc.) are required to be human verified prior to action.
overview: Kensington CA PD uses Flock Safety technology to capture objective evidence without compromising on individual privacy. Kensington CA PD utilizes retroactive search to solve crimes after they've occurred. Additionally, Kensington CA PD utilizes real time alerting of hotlist vehicles to capture wanted criminals. In an effort to ensure proper usage and guardrails are in place, they have made the below policies and usage statistics available to the public.
additional_info:
Kensington CA PD's full ALPR policy can be found at the following link:

https://www.kppcsd.org/files/4edfe2053/ALPRs+Policy+Approved+02.08.2024.pdf
